cases.title: Recent merged PRs with confirmed production deployment
cases.user_request: Review the last two merged PRs for this service, confirm whether they reached production, and summarize any observable impact.

notes: GitHub PR metadata, deploy logs, and service metrics are available.
The expected output is a short PR-by-PR report.

static_checks: Includes merge and deployment evidence before impact analysis.
review_questions: Does the report tie each production claim back to a specific PR and deployment signal?
Does it compare a before-and-after window instead of making generic statements?
Would an engineer understand whether each PR changed production behavior or not?

cases.title: Deployment status cannot be confirmed
cases.user_request: Tell me whether yesterday's merged changes hit production and what they did.

notes: Deployment evidence is incomplete or missing.

static_checks: Avoids claiming production impact when deployment cannot be confirmed.
review_questions: Does the response stop short of claiming production impact without deploy proof?
Does it state exactly what evidence is missing?
Does it still provide a useful scoped readout rather than giving up entirely?

cases.title: Conflicting metrics after deployment
cases.user_request: Check whether the newest merged PR hurt production after it deployed.

notes: Different monitoring sources disagree about impact.

static_checks: Calls out disagreement across data sources.
review_questions: Does the report compare sources instead of cherry-picking one convenient metric?
Does it avoid overstating causality when the evidence conflicts?
Does it leave the user with a clear next question or follow-up check if uncertainty remains?
